---
title: "{{< var icon.computer-activity >}} Activity"
subtitle: "Discussion 1, {{< var course.code >}}, {{< var course.term >}}"
---

## {{< var icon.task >}} Steps

-   [ ] First step.
-   [ ] Second step.

## {{< var icon.resource >}} Resources


## Learning Goal

Students will practice applying the AI 100 Code of Conduct to realistic AI activities and project ideas.

By the end of the activity, students should be able to classify AI project ideas as Green, Yellow, or Red, and explain how a risky idea could be revised into a safer one.

## Time Estimate

30–40 minutes

## Group Size

Small groups of 3–5 students

## Materials Needed

* [AI 100 Code of Conduct](discussion-01_activity/code-of-conduct.md)
* Scenario cards
* Shared worksheet, discussion board, or group document
* Three labels:
  * Green: likely okay
  * Yellow: ask first or revise
  * Red: not allowed

## Step-by-Step Instructions

### 1. Read the Code Individually, 5 minutes

Read the AI 100 Code of Conduct.

As you read, underline or note:

* One rule that seems clear
* One rule that seems important
* One rule or situation you have questions about

### 2. Discuss the Main Idea as a Group, 5 minutes

In your group, summarize the code in one sentence.

Use this sentence starter:

> In AI 100, responsible AI use means...

### 3. Classify Scenario Cards, 10 minutes

<!-- TODO: complete the scenario classification steps and the remaining activity sections -->
